metadata.philosophy: Founded in 1893, Cantina Terlano is a cooperative of 143 grower-members farming 165 hectares on quartz porphyry volcanic soils. Famous for extreme aging on lees — the Rarity program releases wines after 10+ years on lees. Winemaker Rudi Kofler oversees all production. The unique porphyry terroir gives the wines their distinctive mineral character.

_notes.scope: Complete portfolio of 22 wines across 4 tiers: Primo (1), Rarity (1), Selection (11 — 7 white, 4 red), Tradition (10 — 6 white, 4 red). All wines scraped from cantina-terlano.com.
_notes.appellations: Most wines are Alto Adige Terlano DOC. Exceptions: Santa Maddalena is Alto Adige Santa Maddalena DOC. Lagrein Gries Riserva is Alto Adige Gries DOC. The Sauvignon Asparagus is a special bottling within Alto Adige DOC.
_notes.grapes_note: Torilan lists 'Cabernet' without specifying Sauvignon vs Franc — entered as Cabernet Sauvignon based on Alto Adige convention.

metadata.soil: Light, sandy-loamy soils with 55-60% quartz content and slightly acidic pH; subvolcanic origin with high permeability and limited nutrient supply
metadata.vinification_notes: Fermented in 12 hl oak casks. Malolactic fermentation and aging on lees for 12 months in big wooden barrels.

vintages.winemaker_notes: Color: Brilliant straw yellow. Nose: Rich and complex; citrus fruit, white pepper, herbs, delicate smoky note. Palate: Elegant and perfectly balanced with remarkable structure and subtle texture; extremely long finish.

metadata.soil: Light, sandy-loamy soils with 55-60% quartz content, slightly acidic pH, volcanic origin quartz porphyry bedrock
metadata.vinification_notes: Fermented in stainless steel tanks. Malolactic fermentation, then 12 months in big wooden barrels, followed by 11 years on lees in steel tanks without filtering or fining.

vintages.aging_vessel: big wooden barrels, then steel tanks on lees
vintages.winemaker_notes: Color: Intensive light straw yellow with delicate greenish reflections. Nose: Noble aromas of white flowers, chamomile, white peach with restrained mineral notes reminiscent of flint. Palate: Grippy, compact yet supple structure with infinitely long finish, enormous tension and salty minerality.

wines.name: Nova Domus Riserva
food_pairings: Tripe with filet of veal and asparagus crisps

metadata.soil: Light, sandy-loamy soils with 55-60% quartz content, slightly acidic pH, high permeability, limited nutrient supply
metadata.vinification_notes: Fermented in big oak barrels (30 hl). Partial malolactic fermentation and aging on lees for 12 months in big wooden barrels. Blending three months before bottling.

vintages.winemaker_notes: Color: Fresh light yellow with slightly greenish nuances. Nose: Multifaceted with hints of herbs and spices (aniseed, mint, sage), fruit aromas (apricot, mandarine, honeydew melon, maracuja, passion fruit), salty mineral components. Palate: Complex and delicate, creamy softness with aromatic minerality, long finish.

metadata.soil: Light, sandy-loamy soils with 55-60% quartz content, slightly acidic pH, volcanic origin bedrock
metadata.vinification_notes: Fermented in big oak barrels (70 + 30 hl). Malolactic fermentation and aging on lees for 12 months in traditional wooden barrels.

vintages.winemaker_notes: Color: Sparkling straw yellow. Nose: Ripe fruit (honeydew melon, white peach, pear, Golden Delicious apple), quince jelly, camomile, pineapple, mineral flint notes. Palate: Fruity aromatic and salty interplay with compact structure, elegant creamy elements, lingering minerally spicy character.

wines.name: Quarz
food_pairings: Green apple risotto with crispy fried char filets
Roast lamb in herb crust with poached asparagus
White Terlano asparagus risotto with Tropea onions and balsamico vinegar

metadata.soil: Light, sandy-loamy soils with 55-60% quartz content, slightly acidic pH, volcanic porphyry bedrock origin
metadata.vinification_notes: 50% fermented in big wooden barrels, 50% in stainless steel tanks. Aging on lees for 9 months. Blending three months before bottling.

vintages.winemaker_notes: Color: Brilliant straw yellow. Nose: Exotic with mango, papaya, lime, red grapefruit, lemon grass, lemon balm, mint, green tea, flint minerality, and gooseberry notes. Palate: Fascinating interplay of juicy fruit aromas and delicate minerality creating harmonious opulence with long, impressive finish.

wines.name: Lunare
food_pairings: Sweet and sour Asian cuisine dishes
Squash gnocchi with walnuts and Alba truffles with Balsamico dressing
Mild blue cheese like mild gorgonzola

metadata.soil: Red porphyry (quartz porphyry), igneous rock with large mineral inclusions
metadata.vinification_notes: Fermented in stainless steel tanks and big wooden barrels. Aging on lees for 9 months, 50% in big wooden barrels, 50% in stainless steel tanks. Blending 3 months before bottling.

vintages.winemaker_notes: Color: Intensive light-golden yellow. Nose: Dense, finely knit nose with exotic tropical aromas of grapefruit, lime, lychee and orange with rose leaf and ginger notes. Palate: Fruity aromatic character continues on palate, highly quaffable with classic dry finish, complex yet very salty and smooth.

wines.name: Kreuth
food_pairings: Crepes au gratin with radicchio filling

metadata.soil: Light, sandy-loamy soils with 55-60% quartz content, slightly acidic pH, high permeability, volcanic origin bedrock
metadata.vinification_notes: 50% fermented in stainless steel tanks, 50% in big wooden barrels. Partial malolactic fermentation and aging on lees for 10 months.

vintages.winemaker_notes: Color: Brilliant straw yellow. Nose: Exotic fruit components such as maracuja, star fruit and citrus, with spicy minerally notes of flint. Palate: Supple and firm structure with spicy, mineral and juicy aromas giving distinctive, strong terroir character.

wines.name: Winkl
food_pairings: Boiled white asparagus in Bolzano sauce

metadata.soil: Light, sandy-loamy soils with 55-60% quartz content, slightly acidic pH, high permeability, limited nutrient supply, quartz porphyry bedrock of volcanic origin
metadata.vinification_notes: Fermented in stainless steel tanks. Aging on lees for 7-8 months, 80% in stainless steel tanks, 20% in big wooden barrels.

vintages.winemaker_notes: Color: Intensive straw yellow with delicate green shimmer. Nose: Ripe fruit aromas of apricot, mandarin and passion fruit with elements of elderberry, gooseberry and mint. Palate: Fruitiness continues with fine acidity, good structure, minerally but aromatic finish.

wines.name: Terlaner Classico
food_pairings: Pizza and pasta with tomato sauce

metadata.soil: Light, sandy-loamy soils with 55-60% quartz content, slightly acidic pH, volcanic quartz porphyry bedrock
metadata.vinification_notes: Fermented in stainless steel tanks. Aging on lees for 6-7 months, 80% in stainless steel tanks, 20% in big wooden barrels.

vintages.winemaker_notes: Color: Brilliant pale yellow with green nuances. Nose: Green apple and white peach with fine nuances of lemon balm and mint. Palate: Peach-forward with lively character, multifaceted and well-structured flavor from freshness and minerality with persistent finish.

wines.name: Porphyr Riserva
food_pairings: Pink roasted saddle of venison in walnut crust with root vegetables and red cabbage
Rib of beef braised in Lagrein with mixed polenta and baby vegetables
Braised calf's cheek with Lagrein sauce on celeriac foam

metadata.soil: Porphyry bedrock; diverse soil types characteristic of Alto Adige
metadata.vinification_notes: Fermented in stainless steel tanks. Malolactic fermentation and aging in barriques for 18 months, one-third new barrels. Blending three months before bottling.

vintages.winemaker_notes: Color: Deep impenetrable ruby with violet reflections. Nose: Aromas of morello cherry and bilberry with notes of licorice, coffee, vanilla, clove and black tea. Palate: Harmonious with compact, concentrated multilayered flavors combining juicy fruit with spicy, peppery elements and dark chocolate notes, silky tannins.

wines.name: Gries Riserva
food_pairings: Veal cheeks braised in Lagrein with root vegetables

metadata.soil: Light, sandy-loamy soils with 55-60% quartz content and slightly acidic pH, derived from quartz porphyry bedrock of volcanic origin
metadata.vinification_notes: Fermented in stainless steel tanks. Malolactic fermentation. Aged 12 months, 50% in big wooden barrels and 50% in barriques (one-third new). Blended three months before bottling.

vintages.winemaker_notes: Color: Deep ruby to garnet red with delicate violet shimmer. Nose: Earthy, minerally notes with morello cherry, dark chocolate and plum jam. Palate: Spicy, velvety fruit notes with multilayered opulence and great complexity.

wines.name: Monticol Riserva
food_pairings: Saddle of venison with red wine apple puree and quark spaetzle
Poppy-seed roasted saddle of sucking calf
Loin of beef herb roasted in foil with potato pancakes and chestnut ragout
Salted and smoked venison with cranberry sauce and horseradish

metadata.soil: Light, sandy-loamy soils with 55-60% quartz content and slightly acidic pH. Quartz porphyry volcanic bedrock foundation with high permeability.
metadata.vinification_notes: Fermented in stainless steel tanks. Malolactic fermentation. 12 months aging: 30% in big wooden barrels, 70% in barriques (one-third new). Blending three months before bottling.

vintages.winemaker_notes: Color: Intensive ruby red. Nose: Multifaceted with delicate primary aromas of wild strawberries, raspberry and cherry, blended with restrained elegant spicy notes and flint aromas. Palate: Well balanced with mineral elegance, long and exciting finish.

wines.name: Siebeneich Riserva
food_pairings: Shoulder of kid braised in olive oil with asparagus
Braised leg of lamb with spinach polenta

metadata.soil: Light sandy-loamy soils with quartz porphyry bedrock of volcanic origin; 55-60% quartz content, slightly acidic pH
metadata.vinification_notes: Fermented in stainless steel tanks. Malolactic fermentation. 12 months aging: 50% in big wooden barrels, 50% in barriques (one-third new). Blended 3 months before bottling.

vintages.winemaker_notes: Color: Brilliant deep ruby red. Nose: Primary fruit aromas of blackberry and cranberry with herbal notes of mint and eucalyptus, hint of black pepper. Palate: Sturdy tannin structure with rich fruit aromas displaying complexity, depth and length.

wines.name: Pinot Bianco
food_pairings: Courgette flowers with stracchino cheese or burrata
Grilled scampi, dentex or sea bream

metadata.vinification_notes: Fermented in stainless steel tanks. Aging on the lees in steel tanks for 6-7 months.

vintages.winemaker_notes: Color: Clear pale yellow with a slight greenish shimmer. Nose: Crisp notes of apple and pear mingling with aromas of raspberry and lime, plus a touch of camomile and lemon grass. Palate: Intrigues the palate with the fruit of the pear and quince aromas combined with a salty minerality with persistent aftertaste.

wines.name: Chardonnay
food_pairings: Plateau de fruits de mer with raw prawns and langoustines
Soft Robiola cheese and cheese dumplings

metadata.vinification_notes: Fermented in stainless steel tanks. Aging on the lees in steel tanks for 6-7 months.

vintages.winemaker_notes: Color: Brilliant light straw yellow. Nose: Exotic fruity impressions with nuances of banana, quince, passion fruit, mango and sugar melon plus a hint of lemon balm. Palate: Soft and harmonious with sweetish exotic fruit components and slightly mineral note.

wines.name: Pinot Grigio
food_pairings: South Tyrolean hors d'oeuvres such as smoked bacon
Cured meats like Bundnerfleisch or venison salami
Tender veal and fine grilled fish

metadata.vinification_notes: Fermented in stainless steel tanks. Aging on the lees in steel tanks for 5-7 months.

vintages.winemaker_notes: Color: Intensive straw yellow. Nose: Stimulating fruit reminiscent of Williams pear, lychee and white melon with floral character. Palate: Finely balanced components from black tea aromas to exotic fruity nuances of melon and ripe grapefruit.

wines.name: Gewurztraminer
food_pairings: Sweet and sour wok vegetables with chicken breast or shrimps

metadata.vinification_notes: Fermented in stainless steel tanks. 6-7 months aging on lees in steel tanks.

vintages.winemaker_notes: Color: Full, intensive straw yellow. Nose: Stimulating aroma reminiscent of lychee and mango with delicate honeysuckle notes and rose petal. Palate: Aromatic character with fresh fruit, lively acidity combining with mineral notes for round, firm impression.

metadata.vinification_notes: Fermented in stainless steel tanks. Aging on the lees in steel tanks for 5-7 months.

vintages.winemaker_notes: Color: Delicate pale yellow. Nose: Fine spicy aroma of grapefruit and white pepper with ripe peaches. Palate: Well-balanced structure with lively acidity, delicate and elegant.

metadata.vinification_notes: Fermented in stainless steel tanks. Bottled slightly earlier than standard vintage. Grapes harvested separately.
metadata.note: Sauvignon Asparagus — special bottling designed specifically for pairing with asparagus.

vintages.winemaker_notes: Nose: Delicate fruit of the bouquet and fresh aromas create subtle combinations with the taste of asparagus.

metadata.vinification_notes: Fermented in stainless steel tanks. Aging on the lees in steel tanks for 6-7 months.

vintages.winemaker_notes: Color: Clear, intensively pink with salmon-colored reflections. Nose: Highly fruity with notes of raspberry and strawberry with a hint of amarena cherry. Palate: Very juicy and soft with fresh and fruity acidity, highly harmonious appeal.

wines.name: Lagrein
food_pairings: Braised haunch of venison with red cabbage

metadata.vinification_notes: Fermented in stainless steel tanks. Malolactic fermentation and aging in big wooden barrels for 7-10 months.

vintages.winemaker_notes: Color: Dark garnet red. Nose: Intensive aroma of dried cranberries and morello cherry with floral lilac notes, hints of laurel, and restrained chocolate. Palate: Juicy and harmonious with delicate texture and silky tannins; rare opulence, rich and velvety.

wines.name: Pinot Noir
food_pairings: Roast kid with celeriac and carrot puree

metadata.vinification_notes: Fermented in stainless steel tanks. Malolactic fermentation and aging in big wooden barrels for 7-10 months.

vintages.winemaker_notes: Color: Lively ruby red. Nose: Fresh stimulating nose with blackberry, bilberry, cherry brandy hints, licorice and undergrowth aromas. Palate: Delightful balance between ripe fruit and delicate tannin structure conferring elegance on this minerally red wine.

wines.name: Torilan
food_pairings: Venison medallions in South Tyrolean bread crust with Schupfnudel

metadata.vinification_notes: Fermented in stainless steel tanks. Malolactic fermentation and aging in big wooden barrels for 7-10 months. Blending six months before bottling.

vintages.winemaker_notes: Color: Intensive garnet red with ruby reflections. Nose: Merlot aromas of cassis and blackberry with noble spicy Cabernet character, black pepper and vanilla notes. Palate: Symbiosis of fruit, spiciness and tannins of Cabernet, and rich complexity of Merlot.

wines.name: Santa Maddalena
food_pairings: Cured meats like bacon and Bundnerfleisch
Venison carne salada with potted mushroom and cream cheese mousse
Boiled beef with salsa verde and parsley potatoes

metadata.vinification_notes: Fermented in stainless steel tanks. Malolactic fermentation and aging in big wooden barrels for 7-8 months.

vintages.winemaker_notes: Color: Deep ruby red. Nose: Clear primary aromas of black elderberry and cherry underpinned by bitter almond and violet nuance. Palate: Full fruit flavors, soft and elegant with balanced acidity and low tannin content.
